summary.goal: Handle any markdown file size swiftly — from tiny READMEs to multi-MB exported documentation — with tiered loading, smooth scrolling, and bounded memory
summary.description:
The app must handle any markdown file size swiftly - from tiny READMEs to multi-MB exported documentation.

## Performance Requirements
- Launch to readable content: < 200ms for typical files (< 100KB)
- No perceptible delay for files under 100KB
- Smooth scrolling at 60fps regardless of file size
- No UI freezing during load or render
- Memory usage sub-linear with file size

## Tiered Loading Strategy

### Tier 1: Small (< 50KB) — Instant
- Full parse in single pass (~5ms for 50KB with cmark-gfm)
- Complete AttributedString generated before display
- Entire file laid out by TextKit 2
- Memory: ~5MB total

### Tier 2: Medium (50KB-500KB) — Progressive
- Parse in 10KB chunks on background thread
- First screenful rendered within 200ms budget
- Remaining content appended as parsed
- User sees content immediately, more appears as they scroll
- Memory: ~15-30MB

### Tier 3: Large (500KB-5MB) — Lazy Render
- Stream-parse with cmark-gfm streaming API
- TextKit 2 lazy layout: only lay out visible viewport + 2 screenfuls buffer
- Scroll position estimation for scrollbar accuracy
- Recycle layout fragments outside buffer zone
- Memory: ~50-80MB (capped)

### Tier 4: Very Large (5MB+) — Virtualized
- Memory-mapped file I/O (mmap())
- Parse-on-demand: only parse blocks near viewport
- Estimated total height from byte-count heuristic
- Aggressive layout recycling
- Memory: ~80-120MB hard cap

## Scroll Performance
- Target: 60fps (16.67ms per frame budget)
- Pre-compute layout 2 screenfuls ahead of scroll direction
- Cancel pre-computation on scroll direction change
- Use CADisplayLink for scroll-synchronized updates
- Momentum scrolling must feel native (delegate to NSScrollView)

## Background Thread Architecture
- Parsing: dedicated serial queue (QOS: .userInitiated)
- Styling (AST → AttributedString): can parallelize per-block
- Layout: must happen on main thread (TextKit constraint)
- File I/O: use DispatchIO for efficient buffered reads

## Non-Functional
- CPU usage drops to near-zero when idle (not scrolling)
- No background timers or polling
- App nap compatible when window is occluded
- Energy efficiency: should not appear in Activity Monitor as energy-hungry

acceptance_criteria.description: Files under 100KB load with no perceptible delay
Maintains 60fps scrolling regardless of file size
Large files show first screenful within 200ms
Memory usage stays within specified tiers based on file size
Parsing and styling occur on background threads

observations: Four-tier loading strategy based on file size
cmark-gfm streaming API for large files
TextKit 2 lazy layout handles viewport-only rendering
Layout must happen on main thread (TextKit constraint)
concerns: Scroll position estimation accuracy for very large files

observations: Pre-compute 2 screenfuls ahead of scroll direction
CPU near-zero when idle — no background timers
Styling can parallelize per-block on background threads

steps.title: Implement file size detection and tier routing
steps.description: Add a tier determination step at the start of the pipeline. Check the file size (via FileManager.attributesOfItem) and route to the appropriate loading strategy. Tier 1 (< 50KB): full parse. Tier 2 (50KB-500KB): progressive chunked. Tier 3 (500KB-5MB): stream-parse with lazy layout. Tier 4 (5MB+): mmap with parse-on-demand. The PipelineCoordinator selects the strategy based on tier. This must be nearly instant (< 1ms) since it happens in the launch-critical path.

acceptance_criteria.description: File size checked in < 1ms via FileManager
Correct tier selected for each file size range
PipelineCoordinator uses tier to select appropriate strategy
Tier boundaries: 50KB, 500KB, 5MB

architecture.pattern: Strategy pattern — each tier has a PipelineStrategy

steps.title: Implement Tier 3 stream-parse with lazy layout
steps.description: For files 500KB-5MB, implement stream-parsing with cmark-gfm's streaming API. Feed content in ~10KB chunks via cmark_parser_feed(). After each chunk, style the newly parsed AST nodes and append to NSTextContentStorage. TextKit 2's NSTextLayoutManager only lays out the visible viewport + 2 screenfuls buffer. Content outside this range is laid out lazily as the user scrolls. This should keep memory under 80MB by not holding the entire styled AttributedString in memory.

acceptance_criteria.description: Files 500KB-5MB parsed incrementally in 10KB chunks
First screenful visible within 200ms
Memory stays under 80MB for files up to 5MB
TextKit 2 lazy layout active — only viewport + buffer laid out

steps.title: Implement Tier 4 mmap and parse-on-demand for very large files
steps.description: For files over 5MB, use memory-mapped I/O and parse-on-demand. Memory-map the file via Data(contentsOf:options:.mappedIfSafe). Only parse blocks near the current viewport — use byte offset estimation to determine which portion of the file corresponds to the current scroll position. Estimate total document height from byte count (heuristic: ~25 lines per KB for typical markdown). As the user scrolls, parse and style new regions on-demand. Aggressively recycle styled content outside a 4-screenful window around the viewport. Hard memory cap: 120MB.

acceptance_criteria.description: Files > 5MB use memory-mapped I/O
Only blocks near viewport are parsed and styled
Total height estimated from byte-count heuristic
Memory stays under 120MB hard cap
Scrolling triggers on-demand parsing of new regions

architecture.height_estimation: ~25 lines per KB heuristic, refined as actual content is parsed
architecture.recycling: Clear styled content outside parse window

steps.title: Implement scroll-ahead pre-computation
steps.description: Pre-compute layout ahead of the scroll direction to prevent blank areas appearing during fast scrolling. Detect scroll direction from NSScrollView bounds change notifications. Pre-compute 2 screenfuls of content in the scroll direction on a background queue. Cancel pre-computation when scroll direction reverses. The pre-computation must complete within the 16.67ms frame budget to maintain 60fps. Use a coalescing mechanism to batch rapid scroll events.

acceptance_criteria.description: Scroll direction detected from bounds change notifications
2 screenfuls pre-computed ahead of scroll direction
Pre-computation cancelled on direction change
60fps maintained during scrolling (no blank areas)

architecture.direction: Compare current vs previous scroll offset
architecture.coalescing: DispatchWorkItem with 16ms deadline for batch processing
architecture.pre_compute: Style + append content for 2 screenfuls ahead

steps.title: Implement idle CPU and energy efficiency
steps.description: Ensure the app uses near-zero CPU when idle (not scrolling, no parsing in progress). Remove or disable any background timers, polling, or periodic updates. Ensure animated image GIFs pause when outside viewport (from assets feature). Make the app App Nap compatible: when the window is fully occluded, all background work should suspend. Verify with Activity Monitor and Instruments Energy Log that the app does not appear as energy-hungry.

acceptance_criteria.description: CPU drops to 0% when idle (not scrolling)
No background timers or polling active when idle
App Nap suspends background work when window occluded
Does not appear as energy-hungry in Activity Monitor

architecture.no_timers: No Timer.scheduledTimer or DispatchSource.makeTimerSource when idle
architecture.gif_pause: Animated images pause when outside viewport

steps.title: Add memory monitoring and budget enforcement
steps.description: Implement a MemoryMonitor that tracks total app memory usage and enforces the tier-based budgets. Monitor: text storage size, image cache size, layout fragments, and parsed AST data. When approaching the budget for the current tier, trigger eviction of non-essential data (far-from-viewport images, recycled layout fragments, parsed-but-unstyled AST regions). Log warnings via os_log when memory approaches limits. Never exceed the hard cap (120MB) — if necessary, degrade to lower-fidelity rendering (skip syntax highlighting, reduce image resolution).

acceptance_criteria.description: MemoryMonitor tracks text storage + image cache + layout + AST memory
Eviction triggered when approaching tier budget
Hard cap of 120MB never exceeded
Graceful degradation when memory-constrained
os_log warnings when approaching limits

eviction_order: Syntax highlighting for non-visible code blocks
